summary: Materialized the Batch G epics-and-stories artifact with 3 user-value epics and 14 implementation-ready stories aligned to the approved preview/apply/audit architecture.
decisions: Kept scope constrained to the Batch G brownfield architecture: one selected student enrollment at a time inside the existing admin surface.
Organized the work into three value-based epics: preview workflow, controlled apply, and execution follow-up/stabilization.
Preserved manual_assisted as the active MVP source mode and kept json_file explicitly out of current implementation scope.
Carried forward the approved backend shape: preview/apply under the existing admin student namespace, GET execution detail, and legacy endpoint compatibility during migration.
Kept rollback operator-assisted and documented rather than promoting a primary UI rollback action in Batch G.
risks: The broader discovery brief mentions multiple possible ingestion origins, but this decomposition intentionally freezes Batch G to manual_assisted; widening that scope later will require a new planning decision.
Story 2.4 assumes the legacy carga-inicial endpoint can bridge to the new orchestration without breaking frozen contract expectations; that compatibility should be validated during readiness review.
Execution detail rendering in Story 3.1 is safe only if the final API payload continues to hide internal snapshot paths and other server-only metadata.
